title: Heigh Ho, The Rain
content:
The Lark that in heaven dim
     Can match a rainy hour
     With his own music's shower,
    Can make me sing like him, 
     Heigh ho! The rain!

    Sing, when a Nightingale
     Pours forth her own sweet soul
     To hear dread thunder roll
    Into a tearful tale, 
     Heigh ho! The rain!

    Sing, when a Sparrow's seen
     Trying to lie at rest
     By pressing his warm breast
    To leaves so wet and green, 
     Heigh ho! The rain!
meaning:
William Henry Davies's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Heigh Ho, The Rain"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
